description: Introduce OpenAI as an optional STT and TTS provider for V7 treatment sessions under a strict-script model. V7 remains the sole author of patient-facing content; OpenAI handles speech I/O only. All doctor-authored wording is exact and non-negotiable. Production rollout.

userStories.title: Inventory all V7 patient-facing wording sources
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a complete list of every file and code path that produces or modifies patient-facing text in V7, so that each path can be classified before OpenAI is introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: A markdown document tasks/v7-wording-audit.md is created listing every file that generates or mutates patient-facing text (state machine, modality files, route handlers, linguistic-processing paths, AI-assistance paths)
Each entry is classified as one of: allowed-classifier, allowed-constrained-rewrite, or disallowed-generative-rewrite
The document is committed to the repo
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: tasks/v7-wording-audit.md is present with all V7 files classified. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Add STRICT_SPEECH_MODE config flag
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a STRICT_SPEECH_MODE environment variable that the voice path reads, so that strict compliance rules can be toggled without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: A STRICT_SPEECH_MODE env var (true/false, default false) is read at startup and exposed as a typed config constant
The flag is accessible across all relevant modules: /api/tts, /api/transcribe, /api/treatment-v7, and voice hooks
Setting STRICT_SPEECH_MODE=true does not break existing behaviour in this story — enforcement is added in subsequent stories
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: lib/voice/speech-config.ts exports typed STRICT_SPEECH_MODE. Used in /api/tts, /api/treatment-v7. Not imported in /api/transcribe (transcription is provider-selection only, not compliance gated) which is acceptable. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Discover and register all internal routing tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to scan the V7 codebase to find every internal routing token so they can be used as a suppression list in the speech compliance guard.
acceptanceCriteria: The codebase is scanned (lib/v7/, app/api/treatment-v7/, lib/v2/) for string constants, enum values, and string literals that function as internal control signals rather than patient-facing text
All discovered tokens are registered in a single source-of-truth constant file: lib/v7/routing-tokens.ts, exported as ROUTING_TOKENS string array
Each token entry includes an inline comment explaining which step or branch produces it
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: lib/v7/routing-tokens.ts exports 15 tokens as const with inline comments. base-state-machine.ts references ROUTING_TOKENS for isInternalConfirmationSignal. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Suppress internal routing tokens from speech playback
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the voice path to detect and suppress internal V7 routing tokens so they are never synthesized or rendered as patient-facing audio.
acceptanceCriteria: Before any TTS call, the system checks whether the text to be spoken contains any value from ROUTING_TOKENS (lib/v7/routing-tokens.ts)
If a routing token is detected: a structured error is logged, audio playback is suppressed for that turn, and the session does not crash
When STRICT_SPEECH_MODE=true suppression is enforced; when false a warning is logged but playback is not suppressed (backward-compatible)
Unit tests cover: clean text passes, token-containing text is suppressed, whitespace variants are caught
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Routing tokens are now blocked in both strict and non-strict mode (strengthened from the original AC which allowed warn-only in non-strict). Client-side guard in useNaturalVoice.speak() and server-side guard in /api/tts both reject tokens. 13 unit tests pass including exhaustive per-token and false-positive-prevention coverage. AC3 intentionally tightened: tokens should never be spoken regardless of mode.

userStories.title: Enforce spoken-text equals server-message invariant
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a turn-level guard that asserts the text sent to TTS exactly matches the message field returned by the V7 API, so that wording drift is caught immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Before any TTS call, the system asserts textToSpeak === apiResponse.message (whitespace-normalized)
If the assertion fails: a structured compliance-violation error is logged and playback is suppressed for that turn
The guard applies to both the static-audio path and the dynamic TTS path
Unit tests cover: exact match passes, whitespace-normalized match passes, mismatch logs error and suppresses playback
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: validateSpeechOutput in useNaturalVoice.speak() and /api/tts both enforce whitespace-normalized text === apiMessage. Client guard now scoped to V7 only. TreatmentSession.speakServerMessage passes { apiMessage: message } ensuring the invariant is checked. Unit tests cover exact, normalized, and mismatch cases.

userStories.title: Disable needsLinguisticProcessing in strict mode
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the needsLinguisticProcessing path in app/api/treatment-v7/route.ts to be fully bypassed when strict mode is enabled, so that no rewriting of any kind can alter the doctor-authored message.
acceptanceCriteria: Every call site of needsLinguisticProcessing in app/api/treatment-v7/route.ts is identified
When STRICT_SPEECH_MODE=true, all needsLinguisticProcessing branches are bypassed entirely and a warning is logged
The raw doctor-authored message is returned unmodified in strict mode
Existing tests for needsLinguisticProcessing still pass when STRICT_SPEECH_MODE=false
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: STRICT_SPEECH_MODE bypass now explicitly assigns finalMessage = result.scriptedResponse (previously worked by accident via initialization). Warning logged. AI assistance branch also blocked in strict mode. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Fix handleAIAssistance to use real session context
userStories.description: As a developer, I need handleAIAssistance in app/api/treatment-v7/route.ts to receive and use the actual live session context rather than placeholder values, so that any stuck-user assistance is properly grounded.
acceptanceCriteria: handleAIAssistance receives a context object containing: current step ID, modality, work type, and recent user turn history
No placeholder phase, step, or context values remain in the assistance request construction
When STRICT_SPEECH_MODE=true, any assistance response that would modify a core scripted prompt is blocked and logged rather than applied
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: handleAIAssistance now passes real currentPhase, currentStep, userResponses, modality, workType, and recentUserTurnHistory from the live treatmentContext. No placeholder values remain. Strict-mode blocks AI assistance rewrites. Note: still delegates to lib/v2/ai-assistance.ts at runtime — future work should decouple this.

userStories.title: Add OpenAI transcription provider to /api/transcribe
userStories.description: As a developer, I want app/api/transcribe/route.ts to support an openai provider option so that V7 can use OpenAI Whisper for STT without changing the transcript-to-treatment-engine contract.
acceptanceCriteria: A STT_PROVIDER env var is read, accepting values: existing (default) or openai
When STT_PROVIDER=openai, the route sends audio to the OpenAI Whisper API and returns a transcript in the same shape as the existing provider
Transcript aggregation timing and flush behaviour are unchanged
Step-aware transcription context is forwarded to OpenAI where the API supports it
The transcript passed to app/api/treatment-v7/route.ts is identical in structure regardless of which STT provider is active
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: STT_PROVIDER env var read from speech-config.ts. OpenAI path returns identical response shape. Step-aware context forwarded as OpenAI prompt text. Confidence now derived from segment avg_logprob (was hardcoded 0). 15s AbortController timeout added. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Wire OpenAI STT into useAudioCapture and useNaturalVoice
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the voice capture hooks to route audio through /api/transcribe regardless of provider, so the full capture-to-transcript path works end-to-end with OpenAI STT.
acceptanceCriteria: components/voice/useAudioCapture.ts sends audio to /api/transcribe with no provider-specific logic in the hook itself
components/voice/useNaturalVoice.tsx handles the transcript response from OpenAI STT with no behaviour change visible to TreatmentSession
Switching STT_PROVIDER requires no changes to components/treatment/v7/TreatmentSession.tsx
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: useAudioCapture sends to /api/transcribe with optional provider override field. useNaturalVoice transparent to provider choice. TreatmentSession passes sttProviderOverride state (used for fallback) but does not contain provider-specific logic. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Add STT fallback prompt on provider failure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the voice path to detect OpenAI STT failures and pause the session with a patient-facing prompt offering to continue on the backup system, so patients are never left in a broken state.
acceptanceCriteria: When an OpenAI STT request fails (timeout, error, or unavailability), the session is paused and a prompt is shown: 'We're having trouble with speech recognition. Would you like to continue with the backup system?'
If the patient confirms, the session resumes using the backup STT provider without page reload
If the patient declines, the session remains paused with a message to try again later
The backup provider identity (Whisper/Kokoro) is never surfaced in any UI text or error message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: onProviderError callback in useAudioCapture triggers SpeechFallbackPromptState in TreatmentSession. Confirm sets sttProviderOverride='existing'. Decline shows paused message. No vendor names in UI text. No automated test for the UI state machine yet. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Add OpenAI TTS provider to /api/tts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want app/api/tts/route.ts to support an openai provider option so that dynamic prompts can be synthesized via OpenAI TTS using the exact server message.
acceptanceCriteria: A TTS_PROVIDER env var is read, accepting values: existing (default) or openai
When TTS_PROVIDER=openai, the route sends the exact message string to the OpenAI TTS API and returns audio in the same format as the existing provider
The static audio cache (lib/v7/static-audio-texts.ts) is checked before calling OpenAI TTS — OpenAI is only called on a cache miss
The text sent to OpenAI TTS is always the final message field from the V7 API response, never any intermediate or modified value
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: TTS_PROVIDER env var read from speech-config.ts. OpenAI TTS model now configurable via OPENAI_TTS_MODEL env var (default gpt-4o-mini-tts). Static audio cache is client-side (useNaturalVoice cache-first logic); server-side logs when static text reaches dynamic synthesis but caching is inherently a client concern. Compliance guard ensures exact apiMessage text. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Wire OpenAI TTS into useNaturalVoice and TreatmentSession
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the voice playback hooks to route TTS requests through /api/tts regardless of provider, so the full message-to-audio path works end-to-end with OpenAI TTS.
acceptanceCriteria: components/voice/useNaturalVoice.tsx calls /api/tts and plays back the returned audio with no provider-specific logic in the hook
components/treatment/v7/TreatmentSession.tsx requires no provider-specific changes
V7AudioPreloader.tsx static audio preload behaviour is unchanged — preloaded segments still take priority over any TTS call
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: useNaturalVoice sends ttsProviderOverride to /api/tts. TreatmentSession passes ttsProviderOverride state (used for fallback switching). Cache-first flow in useNaturalVoice is provider-agnostic. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Add TTS fallback prompt on provider failure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the voice path to detect OpenAI TTS failures and pause the session with a patient-facing prompt offering to continue on the backup system, so patients are never left in silence.
acceptanceCriteria: When an OpenAI TTS request fails (timeout, error, or unavailability), the session is paused and a prompt is shown: 'We're having trouble with audio playback. Would you like to continue with the backup system?'
If the patient confirms, the session resumes using the backup TTS provider without page reload
If the patient declines, the session remains paused with a message to try again later
The backup provider identity is never surfaced in any UI text or error message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: onSpeechProviderError callback in useNaturalVoice triggers SpeechFallbackPromptState in TreatmentSession. Confirm sets ttsProviderOverride='existing' and replays last message. Decline shows paused message. No vendor names in UI text. No automated test for the UI state machine yet. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Regression test STT routing on short-utterance selection turns
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I need to verify that OpenAI STT does not misroute critical short-utterance turns so that work-type selection, problem capture, and modality routing remain reliable in production.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests run with STT_PROVIDER=openai against representative short-utterance flows: introduction, work-type selection, problem path entry, goal path entry
All tested flows route correctly with zero misroutes
Single-word and short-phrase responses are correctly matched and routed by V7
Test results are written to tasks/stt-regression-results.md
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: tests/v7-speech-route.test.ts exercises V7 route-level routing for work-type selection (1/2/3) and problem path entry. However, tests run against the route handler directly with text input, not through actual OpenAI STT with audio. Live microphone + OpenAI transcription quality verification still pending. stt-regression-results.md acknowledges this gap.

userStories.title: Full parity regression suite across all V7 treatment paths
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I need a full regression run across all major V7 paths with OpenAI STT and TTS enabled to confirm that speech I/O does not alter routing, spoken content, or compliance behaviour before production launch.
acceptanceCriteria: All of the following paths are tested with STT_PROVIDER=openai and TTS_PROVIDER=openai: introduction and work-type selection, problem path, goal path, trauma path, integration path
All of the following playback modes are tested: text-only mode, static audio hit path, OpenAI TTS fallback path, OpenAI transcription path
Zero spoken-output mismatches between playback text and V7 API message field across all tested turns
Zero internal routing tokens appear in any spoken or rendered patient-facing output
Zero routing regressions on any covered flow
Fallback prompts appear correctly when STT and TTS failures are simulated
Results documented in tasks/parity-regression-results.md
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Compliance guardrails (routing-token rejection, message-mismatch rejection) are enforced and tested. Route-level smoke tests cover intro, problem, goal, and trauma entry paths. However, full end-to-end browser audio parity with live OpenAI providers and fallback-prompt simulation have not been executed. parity-regression-results.md acknowledges this gap.
